reason: Stage ownership, config placement, and avoiding generic shared helpers.
Subtitle assets/audit/state are file-backed durable contracts requiring typed models and UTF-8 persistence.
Existing ASR fallback, word timestamp, clip_timestamps, and subtitle-audit mistakes that must not regress.
Compact warning/log conventions and avoiding secret/raw transcript dumps.
Current subtitle CLI, env, fallback, audit, and downstream SRT contract.
ASS/export/copywriter downstream behavior depends on canonical SRT subtitle text.
Config/helper additions require search-first and reuse of existing patterns.
ASR output flows into stage signals, highlights, copywriter, edit plans, and exporter.
Historical faster-whisper hardening design, cache/dependency/fallback baseline.
